parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following points: • calculation of mass of C and H • calculation of mass of oxygen in X • calculation of moles of C, H and O • calculation of ratio and deduction of empirical formula Alternative method • calculation of moles of CO and H O • calculation of moles of C and H • calculation of moles of O • calculation of ratio and deduction of empirical formula (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation Element C H O mass (g) 13.80 × (12÷44) = 3.764 2.82 × (2÷18) = 0.313 4.70 – (3.764 + 0.313) = 0.623 moles (mol) 3.764 ÷12 = 0.314 0.313 ÷ 1 = 0.313 0.623 ÷ 16 = 0.0389 ratio 0.314÷0.0389= 8.07 0.313÷0.0389 = 8.05 0.0389 ÷0.0389 = 1 C H O Correct empirical formula with some working scores 4 marks Empirical formula alone just scores M4 Allow TE throughout 13.8 ÷ 44 = 0.31364 (mol) and 2.82 ÷ 18 = 0.15667 (mol) moles of C = 0.31364 (mol) and moles H = (0.15667 × 2) = 0.31333 (mol) 4.70 – [(0.31364 × 12) + (0.31333 × 1)] = 0.62299 (g)
